title: Fix Active Accounts Page — Show Submitted Bank Accounts with Status Indicators

requirements.summary: Remove authentication errors from Active Accounts page and allow public access without login
acceptanceCriteria: Active Accounts page loads without any error message
No authentication or login is required to view the accounts page
Backend does not return unauthorized error when fetching bank accounts

file_operations.description: Remove any authentication checks that prevent loading the page. Update the page to call the public backend method getAllBankAccounts() instead of any user-specific method. Handle the case where the backend returns a list of BankDetailsWithoutNetBanking objects with status field (pending, rejected, liveActive). Remove error handling for unauthorized access and display accounts without requiring authentication.

Add a new React Query hook useGetAllBankAccounts that calls actor.getAllBankAccounts() to fetch all bank accounts without authentication. This hook should not depend on Internet Identity or any authentication state and should be enabled immediately when the actor is available.

requirements.summary: Display bank accounts as cards with Bank Name, Account Type, and masked Account Number (last 4 digits)
acceptanceCriteria: Each bank account is shown as a card with Bank Name, Account Type, and masked Account Number
Account number shows only last 4 digits (e.g., ****1234)
All submitted accounts are listed on the page

file_operations.description: Redesign the page to display each BankDetailsWithoutNetBanking object as a card. Extract Bank Name from the bankName field. Since BankDetailsWithoutNetBanking does not have an explicit accountType field, derive it from available data or display 'Savings' as default. Mask the accountNumber field to show only the last 4 digits (e.g., ****1234) using a helper function. Layout cards in a responsive grid with clear visual separation between accounts.

Create a utility function maskAccountNumber(accountNumber: string): string that returns a masked version showing only the last 4 digits (e.g., '123456789012' becomes '****9012'). Handle edge cases for account numbers shorter than 4 digits.

requirements.summary: Show colored status dots on each account card (Yellow=Pending, Green=Live Active, Red=Rejected)
acceptanceCriteria: Each account card displays a colored status dot
Newly submitted accounts show a yellow (Pending) dot by default
Accounts with 'active' status show a green dot
Accounts with 'rejected' or 'error' status show a red dot
Status dot is clearly visible on the card

file_operations.description: Add a status indicator to each account card. Map the status field from BankDetailsWithoutNetBanking (pending, liveActive, rejected) to colored dots: pending → yellow dot, liveActive → green dot, rejected → red dot. Display the dot prominently on each card along with a text label (e.g., 'Pending', 'Live Active', 'Rejected'). Use Tailwind CSS classes for the colored dots (e.g., bg-yellow-500, bg-green-500, bg-red-500) with appropriate sizing and positioning.

requirements.summary: Newly submitted accounts from BankDetailsForm appear immediately in Active Accounts page with Pending status
acceptanceCriteria: After submitting bank details from any page, navigating to the Accounts tab shows the new account
Newly submitted account appears with Pending (yellow) status
No page refresh required — account appears immediately after submission

file_operations.description: After successfully calling saveCompleteBankDetails in the mutation's onSuccess callback, invalidate the 'allBankAccounts' query key to trigger a refetch of the bank accounts list. Ensure the success toast is shown and the form reflects the submission. The new account should have a default status of 'pending' set by the backend.

Ensure the useGetAllBankAccounts hook has a queryKey of ['allBankAccounts'] so that invalidateQueries can target it. Configure the query to refetch automatically when invalidated to ensure the Active Accounts page updates immediately after form submission.

requirements.summary: Show friendly empty state message when no bank accounts exist
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state message is shown when no accounts exist
Empty state does not show any error message
Empty state includes a prompt or hint to add bank details

file_operations.description: Add a conditional rendering block that checks if the accounts array is empty after successfully loading. If empty, display a friendly empty state message such as 'No accounts added yet. Submit your bank details to get started.' with an optional icon or illustration. Ensure this empty state is visually distinct from error states and provides a clear call-to-action hint.
